notes: Early-WotC booster (June 1999, base2). 11 cards/pack: 7 common, 3 uncommon, 1 rare-or-better. Holo seeded ~1 in 3 packs, matching Base Set's 3:1 ratio. Confirmed by Bulbapedia, PSA Set Registry and Flipside Gaming. Bulbapedia explicitly notes WotC added Non-Holofoil prints of the 16 Holo Rares specifically so packs would NOT guarantee a holo, preserving the 1:3 seeding while enlarging the 64-card set — this is exactly why the set has parallel rare-holo(16) and rare(16) buckets. No reverse-holo slot existed pre-2002, so no reverse slot is modeled. Rare slot resolves to rare-holo 1/3 and non-holo rare 2/3. Booster box = 36 packs; no ETB existed in this era. Confidence medium: the 1:3 holo rate is a well-documented WotC-era figure (printed on packs) rather than a large per-set opened-pack study, but it is corroborated across multiple independent sources. Vintage sealed boxes carry a large collector premium, so EV/booster is expected to sit far below box price — rates are NOT inflated to compensate.
